title: Bounded Operations-Web Vercel Prestage And Preview Redeploy Under Render Block
objective: Advance the hosted lane as far as possible without Render credentials by targeting the real `apex-operations-web` Vercel project, staging `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` there, and validating a server-side preview redeploy while preserving truthful blockage on the missing public mutation-seam host.
decision_rationale: Packet 031 proved the remaining public blocker is the missing Render-authenticated path to create or configure `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com`, but the operations-web Vercel side was still executable from this environment and should be advanced instead of left idle.

The first Vercel env write landed on the wrong project (`apex-power-ops-platform`), so the next truthful slice was to identify the actual production alias owner for `https://operations.apexpowerops.com` and move the seam URL staging onto that real project.

A server-side Vercel redeploy can exercise the new environment variables without depending on the local parent-root packaging path, which is useful because local CLI deploys from `C:/APEX Platform` currently fail before build with a rootDirectory error.
Preview protection keeps preview URLs from serving as the public proof gate, so successful preview redeploy can only count as Vercel-side pre-stage, not as public PM live-data proof.
execution_summary: Confirmed `https://operations.apexpowerops.com` is served by the Vercel project `apex-operations-web`, not by the separate `apex-power-ops-platform` project.

Created an isolated Vercel link outside the repo at `C:/APEX Platform/tmp/vercel-apex-operations-web-link`, targeted it to `apex-operations-web`, and staged `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL=https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com` across production, preview, and development on that real project.
Cleaned the mistakenly added `MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` entries back out of the unrelated `apex-power-ops-platform` Vercel project so only the real browser host retains the new seam env.

Triggered a server-side redeploy of the newest ready preview deployment and validated the new preview URL `https://apex-operations-697hr5p9p-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app`, while separately proving that local CLI deploys from `C:/APEX Platform` still fail because Vercel uploads only `.vercelignore` and then reports the configured rootDirectory as missing.

validation.production_alias_owner: npx -y vercel inspect operations.apexpowerops.com --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects -> PASS (`apex-operations-web`, deployment `dpl_3rR9YukiwRwnMcdbKN87Hh87rpVr`)
validation.correct_project_envs: npx -y vercel env ls --cwd C:/APEX Platform/tmp/vercel-apex-operations-web-link --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects -> PASS (`MUTATION_SEAM_BASE_URL` present on Production, Preview, Development)
validation.wrong_project_cleanup: npx -y vercel env ls from C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform -> PASS (`No Environment Variables found`)
validation.server_side_preview_redeploy: npx -y vercel redeploy https://apex-operations-fej01pnsg-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app --target preview --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects -> PASS (`https://apex-operations-697hr5p9p-jasonlswenson-sys-projects.vercel.app`)
validation.preview_probe: preview PM shell and same-origin API routes -> `401` under preview protection (`/pm-review/index.html`, `/api/v1/reads/approval-queue`, `/api/v1/schedule/projects`)
validation.local_cli_deploy_probe: npx -y vercel deploy --yes --scope jasonlswenson-sys-projects --debug from C:/APEX Platform -> FAIL (uploads only `.vercelignore`, then build reports rootDirectory `apex-power-ops-platform/apps/operations-web` does not exist)

next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 033 - Credentialed Public Mutation-Seam Deployment And Public PM Live-Data Proof
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as a bounded Vercel-side preparation slice after Packet 031. Packet 032 truthfully advances the browser-host side of the lane, but it does not claim that the public mutation-seam host exists, that the production alias was re-promoted with the new seam env, or that public PM live-data proof has passed.
next_action: Use Render-authenticated access to create or configure `https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com`, validate it with the deployed seam smoke, then use the already staged `apex-operations-web` Vercel environment to redeploy and, if valid, promote the browser host before rerunning public PM live-data proof.
